stories.title: CJ Cup Byron Nelson Starts Tomorrow — Scheffler Defends, Rai Withdraws
stories.angle: Scheffler defending at a course he absolutely owns — with a newly redesigned layout specifically built to challenge him. And Rai's withdrawal after his PGA Championship triumph is a major late-breaking story.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The CJ Cup Byron Nelson starts TOMORROW at TPC Craig Ranch.

Scottie Scheffler won last year at 31-under (PGA Tour 72-hole record). Now he returns to defend at a $25M renovated course designed to challenge him.

Spieth and Koepka also in field. 🏌️ #ByronNelson #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
BREAKING: Aaron Rai has withdrawn from the CJ Cup Byron Nelson.

The PGA Champion — who won at 290-1 odds just FOUR DAYS AGO — will not play this week. S.Y. Noh takes his place.

He earned the rest. What a week. #PGATour

article.headline: CJ Cup Byron Nelson Preview: Scheffler Returns to Defend as Rai Withdraws Following PGA Championship Win

stories.title: McIlroy's Grand Slam Bid Falls Short — Eyes Turn to Shinnecock Hills
stories.angle: The calendar Grand Slam remains unfinished. McIlroy's reaction and US Open preparation are the next chapter.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy finished T7 at the PGA Championship. Five shots back.

He had the Masters. He came to Aronimink for a calendar Grand Slam shot.

It didn't happen.

Next chance: US Open. Shinnecock Hills. June 18. #USOpen #McIlroy

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
"Not birdieing the two par 5s and making the bogey at 13."

Rory McIlroy on why his PGA fell apart on Sunday.

He had the round in his hands. It slipped away. Eyes are already on Shinnecock Hills in June. #McIlroy

article.headline: McIlroy's Grand Slam Bid Falls Short at Aronimink — Now the Focus Shifts to Shinnecock Hills

stories.title: Rai Withdraws from Byron Nelson — PGA Champion Takes a Breather
stories.angle: Understandable decision after a historic week — but a notable story for the field and for fans who wanted to watch the PGA champion immediately defend momentum.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Imagine winning the PGA Championship at 290-1 odds.

Then pulling out of next week's event before the ink is dry.

That's Aaron Rai. He withdrew from Byron Nelson — and honestly? He's earned every second of it. #PGAChampionship #PGATour

article.headline: Aaron Rai Withdraws from Byron Nelson — The PGA Champion Takes a Deserved Rest

stories.title: Lottie Woad Wins Queen City — England's Double-Major Moment
stories.angle: Woad's rapid rise; England's remarkable double; who is Lottie Woad?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
England won both sides of golf this week.

Aaron Rai won the PGA Championship (290-1 odds).
Lottie Woad won the LPGA Queen City Championship (her 2nd title in 10 months, at age 22).

What a week to be English. 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿⛳ #LPGATour

article.headline: Lottie Woad Wins Queen City Championship as England Claims a Historic Double in Golf

stories.title: US Open 2026 — Six Weeks Out, Shinnecock Hills Ready
stories.angle: Countdown feature — Shinnecock Hills history, 2026 storylines, and what to expect.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
6 WEEKS UNTIL THE US OPEN. 🏆

June 18-21. Shinnecock Hills, Southampton, NY.

Aaron Rai is in (PGA Champion exemption). McIlroy goes again for the Grand Slam. 200,000 fans expected.

Qualifying continues through June 8. #USOpen #Golf

article.headline: Six Weeks Out: What to Know About the 2026 US Open at Shinnecock Hills
